verdict: Moderate risk of AI displacement, with some tasks becoming more routine or automatable.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine Cognitive Tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks 1, 2, and 4 from the O*NET task list involve preparing documents, which can be automated or heavily assisted by AI tools.

reasoning_steps.title: AI Capability Gap
reasoning_steps.body: Task 3 involves researching law of cases and searching pertinent sources, but current frontier models like Claude can already perform similar tasks with high accuracy.

evidence_ledger.contribution: Routine cognitive tasks and AI capability gap
Understanding of paralegal role and market demand

adjacent_careers.name: Lawyer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both roles require legal knowledge, but lawyers have more expertise and autonomy.

adjacent_careers.name: Legal Assistant
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Paralegals often work under the supervision of lawyers or other professionals, but their tasks can be more routine and automatable.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered document preparation tool launched
recent_developments.impact: This tool can automate some routine document preparation tasks, potentially displacing paralegals in certain roles.

recent_developments.headline: AI contract review reaches enterprise adoption
recent_developments.impact: Top-100 law firms report cutting document review hours by 60–80% on standard agreements.

quotes.quote: Lawyers who use AI will replace lawyers who do not. The profession is not going away, but the structure of how legal work gets done is going to change in a very significant way.
quotes.speaker: Richard Susskind
quotes.title: Author, Tomorrow's Lawyers; technology adviser to the Lord Chief Justice of England

quotes.quote: Document review, due diligence, and first-draft contract work are the parts of legal practice most exposed. Counseling and courtroom judgment are the most insulated.
quotes.speaker: Daniel Susskind
quotes.title: Author, A World Without Work; Oxford economist

quotes.quote: We expect roughly 44 percent of legal work tasks to be automatable by current LLMs. That does not mean 44 percent fewer lawyers — it means restructured firms.
quotes.speaker: Goldman Sachs Research
quotes.title: Economic research note
quotes.source: Goldman Sachs: AI Exposure of Occupations
